title: LibERC7579 mode encoding and batch decoding with bounded calldata pointer validation
trigger_patterns: EIP-7579 batch decoding with bounds checks
explanation: Three operations: (1) encodeMode packs a (callType, execType, selector, payload) tuple into a single bytes32 via three OR-shift compositions — callType in bits [248..255], execType in [240..247], selector in [208..239], payload in [16..207]. byte(0, x) extracts the leftmost byte of a 32-byte word; shr(224, selector) right-aligns the 4-byte selector; shr(80, payload) right-aligns the 22-byte payload. (2) decodeSingle reads target as an address from the first 20 bytes, value from the next 32, then data as the tail — bounds-checking that executionData.length > 51 (0x33). The bound is exactly enough to ensure the address+value fit. (3) decodeBatch performs full ABI bounds checking inline: shr(64, u) ensures the head offset doesn't overflow when shifted, the loop walks each Call's offset+inner-data-offset+inner-data-length and validates none point past the end e (= last 32 bytes of executionData). The combined `or(shr(64, or(calldataload(o), or(p, q))), or(gt(add(c, 0x40), e), gt(add(o, calldataload(o)), e)))` is one OR-tree that any single overflow or out-of-bounds condition triggers.
when_to_apply: Smart accounts implementing EIP-7579 modular execution, ERC7821 batch executors, account abstraction routers handling multiple call modes
when_not_to_apply: Trusted-input contexts where the executionData is constructed by the contract itself — use decodeBatchUnchecked for those
